response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with deep software-craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and genuine low-latency, multi-threaded, networked systems experience via DLang microservices handling high request volumes. However, the role centers on current C++/STL/OOP expertise, and the candidate's C/C++ work (NI LabWindows/CVI, a C++ bachelor thesis) is dated and not listed among present-day skills, which lean heavily toward TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of IEC 62443/SDL security practice, MQTT, industrial protocols, or message-broker architectures. Fundamentals transfer well, but core stack and domain alignment are weak, so an interview is only justifiable if the team values strong generalists who can ramp into C++.
strengths: Deep performance-systems experience: low-latency, multi-threaded, async I/O microservices at scale
Strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based, mentoring/teaching)
Broad full-stack and embedded exposure including some prior C/C++ work
concerns: C++ is not a current core skill (absent from skills list; most C/C++ work is dated)
No IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or industrial-cybersecurity/protocol experience
No message-broker or industrial-automation/IoT domain background
